findings.claim: An explicit structural/geometric prior earns its place ONLY where (a) the base mechanism lacks the signal AND (b) the prior's op can EXPRESS the constraint. Redundant or harmful everywhere else.
findings.source_experiment: Neocognitron arc (N2b, N3, entropy-top, P2, P3, P4)
findings.confidence: high (6 phases, both signs of each condition observed)
findings.scope: prior-vs-base A/Bs in this framework
findings.limitations: controlled analytic tasks; not yet stress-tested on cluttered real data
findings.framework_consequence: Every future prior-vs-base experiment proposal must state which of (a)/(b) it expects to satisfy and how it will test each.
open_questions: does the law hold with nonlinear per-edge messages?

findings.claim: Under a LEARNABLE S-cell, the explicit rotation-invariant C-cell is REDUNDANT: the learnable conv discovers an orientation-agnostic invariant itself (C8 ~= C1), even on a task that forces oriented features (energy baseline at chance 0.47, S-cell 0.73).
findings.scope: single learnable S/C block with a global-mean readout
findings.limitations: does not apply to compositional/deep regimes (see F-N3-1)
findings.framework_consequence: Do not add the C-cell for rotation-invariance under a single learnable block expecting a win; it is a no-op there.

findings.claim: In a COMPOSITIONAL deep regime the C-cell becomes LOAD-BEARING: C8 fits corner-vs-bar (train 1.000, all seeds), C1 cannot fit (train ~chance). The compositional structure defeats the N2b implicit-invariance dodge.
findings.scope: 2-block stack, compositional oriented task
findings.limitations: does not by itself give global rotation generalization (F-N3-2)
findings.framework_consequence: ScBlock C-cell documented as load-bearing in compositional/can't-fit regimes.

findings.claim: LOCAL per-feature orientation-invariance (group_pool C-cell) is NOT global rotation-invariance of a spatial configuration: held-out-rotation AUROC hugs chance for both depths, because a downstream spatial conv still sees a globally-rotated pattern.
findings.scope: S/C stack with a spatial readout
findings.framework_consequence: group_pool docstring + registry record the local/global distinction; global rotation-invariance requires the entropy top.

findings.claim: The response-weighted covariance EIGEN-ENTROPY is a globally-rotation-invariant AND arrangement-sensitive descriptor: it closes the N3 gap (held-out-rotation recognition AUROC 1.000, 5 seeds), the SAME covariance's principal angle is a rotation-equivariant pose readout (0.9deg MAE), and the update is real-time (~300us, ~3300/s). Confirms the user's Neocognitron hypothesis.
findings.scope: controlled 2-shape task; mechanism validated not scaled
findings.limitations: needs oriented S-cell upstream (F-ENT-2); not clutter-tested
findings.framework_consequence: global_entropy_pool is CANONICAL and the recommended rotation-invariant top; supersedes the arrangement-blind channel-mean top.
open_questions: multi-object per-region entropy pools

findings.claim: An entropy-pool top has a COLD-START failure: a random conv gives a near-isotropic covariance -> uninformative Hs gradient -> the S-cell never forms edges and training stalls (2/5 seeds stuck at chance). Warm-starting the S-cell oriented fixes it (5/5 reach target).
findings.scope: any ScBlock feeding global_entropy_pool from a cold start
findings.framework_consequence: GUARD assimilated: `oriented_sobel_bank` / `ScBlock::new_oriented` extracted to framework; entropy-pool docstring and this finding cross-reference. Future entropy-pool pipelines must warm-start the S-cell oriented.

findings.claim: The skeleton hg_conv is NEUTRAL when the occluded joint is over-constrained (recoverable from local visible structure): a 2-link arm's middle joint is recovered by the backbone alone; the P1 unblock (multi-pose localization ~1.7px, no coord channels) is the real positive.
findings.sub_type: scoped-neutral (base mechanism satisfies signal; fails F-ARC-1 condition a)
findings.scope: tree skeleton, locally-recoverable joint
findings.limitations: occlusion-box-shape can leak a bracketed joint (see F-P4-2)
findings.framework_consequence: Do not expect the skeleton to help a locally over-constrained joint; needs redundant long-range structure (F-P3-1).

findings.claim: The skeleton hg_conv is DECISIVE when a redundant structural constraint exists AND the recovery is expressible: coupled arms (L=R+offset), a single-neighbour translation the shared elin expresses exactly. Occluded-arm recovery 2.6 vs 6.9px; left/right disambiguation 5.9 vs 24px (5 seeds).
findings.scope: redundant coupling with translation recovery
findings.limitations: shared elin cannot express reflection/asymmetric closure (F-P4-1)
findings.framework_consequence: skeleton_hg_refine registered as MECHANISM_DEMO; canonical extraction deferred to the per-edge transform (see F-P4-1).
open_questions: reflection symmetry needs a per-edge transform

findings.claim: The signed hg_conv with a SINGLE SHARED elin CANNOT express an asymmetric affine loop closure (C=B+D-A): on the loop edges it collapses recovery to the neighbour MIDPOINT (measured oracle 6.5px), so a closed-loop skeleton HURTS the clean coupler (10.7 vs 7.3px, 5 seeds).
findings.scope: closed kinematic loop / asymmetric neighbour combination
findings.framework_consequence: GUARD/record: skeleton_hg_refine kept NON-CANONICAL for loops; measured spec for the next op = PER-EDGE TRANSFORM (each hyperedge owns its learned map). Do NOT extract the shared-elin refine as canonical.
open_questions: build per-edge transform op; re-run P4 to confirm loop flips hurts->wins
general (nonlinear) 4-bar closure may need a per-edge nonlinearity

findings.claim: An occlusion box drawn to span a joint bracketed by visible neighbours LEAKS that joint's position via the hole shape (P2 elbow, P4 coupler ~1.7px 'recovered' regardless of method). Such occluded numbers are confounded and must not be used as a structural-recovery verdict.
findings.scope: occlusion-robustness evaluation for keypoints
findings.framework_consequence: Evaluation rule: to test structural recovery, occlude a WHOLE separate substructure (P3-style) or verify the box does not bracket the target; rest the verdict on an unconfounded (e.g. clean, or cross-structure) comparison.

findings.claim: Forgetting-RLS (lambda<1) with many features diverges from covariance windup: the precision matrix inflates by 1/lambda per step in poorly-excited directions until a sample hits them and the weights explode (measured RMSE ~1e4).
findings.scope: any EvolventHead with lambda<1 and un-excited feature directions
findings.framework_consequence: EvolventHead carries a covariance-trace guard on by default (bounds trace(P) -> bounds gain). GUARD assimilated in the shared component.
code_changes: src/online.rs p_trace_max + with_trace_cap + trace scaling in update
open_questions: directional forgetting removes windup without a trace cap

findings.claim: The evolvent (exact one-pass forgetting-RLS) is more SAMPLE-EFFICIENT at cold-start than online-SGD: it reaches lower error in the first window on all 5 seeds (0.34 vs 0.46) and ties a backprop-MLP. But it is O(d^2) PER UPDATE (slower than backprop O(d)); 'fast' = sample-fast, not FLOP-fast.
findings.scope: online linear-in-features readout
findings.limitations: advantage is at cold-start / low-data; washes out on long stationary segments
findings.framework_consequence: The evolvent's value is data-efficiency and adaptation, not per-update compute; benchmark it in low-data / rapid-drift regimes.

findings.claim: Plain forgetting-RLS is NOT robustly competitive with backprop-Adam on a long non-stationary stream: a windup-stable config (high lambda + tight cap) under-tracks (steady RMSE 1.6 vs backprop 0.3), while an aggressive config diverges. The strong hypothesis (evolvent beats slow backprop outright) is unsupported by plain RLS.
findings.scope: long-regime non-stationary streaming with plain (isotropic-forgetting) RLS
findings.limitations: E0 task has long segments where SGD converges; does not test the rapid-drift regime
findings.framework_consequence: EvolventHead is EXPERIMENTAL not CANONICAL; do NOT default an evolvent readout as 'beats backprop'. Path to CANONICAL = directional-forgetting variant + rapid-drift benchmark (E1).
open_questions: directional/selective forgetting for windup-free aggressive tracking
rapid-drift task where sample-efficiency is decisive
deeper evolvent (local closed-form beyond the readout)

findings.claim: On STATIC supervised datasets (generated + real, cls + reg), the one-pass evolvent (RLS on a fixed RFF basis) is COMPETITIVE with a 200-epoch backprop MLP: over 6 datasets it WINS 2 (multi-class classification: gen_cls 0.950 vs 0.934, gen_cls_multi 0.995 vs 0.977), LOSES 2 (hard high-d regression gen_reg_highd 0.326 vs 0.408; iris 0.947 vs 0.961), TIES 2 (gen_reg, california). Beats one-pass SGD on all 6. ~1/10 training cost, no lr/epoch/arch tuning. 50 seeds, kato15.
findings.correction: The first run (commit a-side) used a buggy RNG (inputs biased to [-1,0)); the 'matches/beats 5/6, decisive on hard regimes' claim was a data artifact and is RETRACTED. Fixed RNG (>>32/2^32, commit 1d63e68) re-run gives the competitive verdict above; the hard high-d regression REVERSES (MLP wins).
findings.source_experiment: evolvent E1 (examples/evolvent_bench.rs, 50 seeds, kato15 32-core, FIXED RNG)
findings.scope: static supervised learning with a fixed expressive (RFF) basis; multi-output via one-hot RLS
findings.limitations: fixed RFF basis underfits hard high-d nonlinear targets -> learned-feature MLP wins there; long-drift streaming remains F-EVO-3
findings.framework_consequence: EvolventHead DEPLOYABLE for static supervised readouts on the strength of COMPETITIVE accuracy + one-pass + no-tuning (not superiority). Multi-output (classification) canonical. Bug fixed in evolvent_stream/bench/hypergraph RNG.
open_questions: Nagare op basis (HSiKAN/rotor) instead of RFF -- lift on the hard high-d / california?
directional-forgetting streaming (F-EVO-3)

findings.claim: The dense PAIRWISE precision is both a cost (O(d^2)) and a REPRESENTATION limit: a genuine 3-way hyperedge interaction x_a x_b x_c is L2-orthogonal to all pairwise features, so a pairwise model provably cannot capture it (measured R2 ~0.15 on a 3-way-dominant target, 5 seeds). A HYPEREDGE-CLIQUE (block-structured) precision -- one small b_e x b_e block per hyperedge with a shared residual -- recovers it: block R2 0.997 == dense-hyperedge 0.997 at 20x less precision storage (980 vs 19600 nnz), on both disjoint and overlapping hypergraphs.
findings.scope: additive-over-hyperedges targets with bounded-width structure; block precision = block-diagonal RLS
findings.limitations: block precision drops cross-block (separator) coupling -- negligible for additive-over-hyperedge targets here, but would matter for non-additive cross-hyperedge structure; the higher-order term must be an explicit feature
findings.framework_consequence: New component BlockEvolventHead (O(d*w)) registered. The alternative to the pairwise item: hyperedge-clique precision keeps higher-order + cuts cost. Ties the evolvent line to the signed-hypergraph substrate (hg_message, SBSH bounded width).
open_questions: junction-tree (block-tridiagonal) precision for genuine cross-hyperedge coupling
update the block precision THROUGH hg_message (higher-order tensor apply)
SBSH width certificate as the tractability guarantee

findings.claim: The INFORMATION FORM (junction-tree) evolvent -- accumulate J = Phi^T Phi + ridge*I and b = Phi^T y, solve J w = b -- is EXACT (= dense RLS, keeps ALL cross-hyperedge/separator coupling) while J is SPARSE for local (bounded-width) measurements. Measured (kato15, 5 seeds x 4 sizes, local-window chain): INFO R2 == DENSE R2 to printed precision at EVERY (n,seed) cell; info-matrix storage 21.6% -> 11.1% -> 5.6% -> 2.8% of dense as n 48->96->192->384 (halves per doubling: nnz(J)=O(n*w) vs dense O(n^2)). Block-diagonal (E3) trails by <=0.0003 -- it drops the separator coupling INFO keeps exactly. This is the exact version of the E3 path and realises the hypergraph<->tensor conjugation: J IS the signed-hypergraph incidence made numerical (nonzero J[i,k] == GMRF conditional-dependence edge).
findings.source_experiment: evolvent E4 (examples/evolvent_junction.rs, kato15 5 seeds x 4 n)
findings.confidence: high (5 seeds x 4 sizes; INFO==DENSE is an algebraic identity, the run confirms the implementation)
findings.scope: local / bounded-width measurements (sparse J); linear-in-features single-output readout. NOT a win for dense/global measurements (J fills -> same O(d^2) as dense).
findings.limitations: sparsity is a property of the DATA not the head; current solve is dense Gaussian elimination O(d^3) -- the O(d*w^2) block-tridiagonal Cholesky that exploits nnz is NOT yet implemented (E4 measures the STORAGE + EXACTNESS win, not a solve-TIME win); InfoEvolventHead is a CLASSICAL primitive (information filter / Lauritzen-Spiegelhalter junction tree) -- no algorithmic-novelty claim, the framework value is the exact+sparse evolvent head + hypergraph conjugation.
findings.framework_consequence: New component InfoEvolventHead (DEPLOYABLE for local-measurement structure) registered. Completes the evolvent precision family: dense P O(d^2) exact -> block-diagonal O(d*w) approximate -> information J O(d*w) EXACT. Answers the user's 'make P sparse and tensor-like -- conjugation between hypergraph and tensor interface'.
open_questions: sparse block-tridiagonal Cholesky O(d*w^2) -- turn the storage win into a solve-time win
a NON-additive cross-hyperedge target where BLOCK loses materially and INFO/dense hold (the discriminating block-vs-info test)
update J THROUGH hg_message on the signed-hypergraph substrate
SBSH bounded-width certificate as the solve tractability guarantee

findings.claim: The MULTIFRONTAL (clique-tree) Cholesky driven by the hypergraph -- eliminate each clique's residual leaves->root, send the Schur complement over its separator up to the parent (the message = a tensor contraction over the eliminated interior onto the separator boundary), back-substitute root->leaves -- is EXACT (= dense Cholesky of the assembled J) at storage O(sum|C|^2)=O(d*w^2) and factorization O(sum|C|^3)=O(d*w^3). Measured (kato15, 5 seeds x 4 depths, branching binary clique tree): MF R2 == DENSE R2 at every (depth,seed); factorization flop speedup 18x->77x->314x->1270x as d 105->889 (grows (d/w)^2); storage 10.7%->1.3% of dense. An online update touches EXACTLY its clique's path to the root (proven by regression test: changed factors == ancestors_inclusive(leaf)) -> mean path = depth = log2(N) cliques, so incremental re-fire is O(log N * w^3) not O(N * w^3). This is the SOLVE-TIME realization of the E4 information form -- the corner of the precision family that is exact AND cheap-to-solve AND cheap-to-update-online.
findings.source_experiment: evolvent E5 (examples/evolvent_multifrontal.rs, kato15 5 seeds x 4 depths)
findings.confidence: high (5 seeds x 4 depths; MF==DENSE and update-locality are algebraic identities the runs + tests confirm)
findings.scope: bounded-width (junction-tree) SPD precision; a valid clique tree (RIP, separator subset of parent) is required; single-output linear-in-features. The BLOCK gap is small here (~0.0002) BECAUSE the target is additive-over-cliques and data-rich per clique (regime property, not a method limit) -- the data-scarce / non-additive discriminating target is still open.
findings.limitations: the flop win is an ANALYTIC count (Sum|C|^3 vs d^3/6) not a wall-clock benchmark (needs contiguous-storage rewrite + criterion at large d); the incremental re-fire is PROVEN-local but the optimized refactorize_path is NOT built (measure-before-optimize); multifrontal is a CLASSICAL method (Duff-Reid 1983 / junction-tree elimination) -- no algorithmic-novelty claim, framework value = the hypergraph clique tree AS the assembly tree and separators AS messages.
findings.framework_consequence: New component JunctionTreeCholesky (DEPLOYABLE) registered. Completes the evolvent precision family: dense O(d^2)/O(d^3) exact -> block-diagonal O(d*w) approx -> information O(d*w) exact-but-dense-solve -> MULTIFRONTAL O(d*w^2)/O(d*w^3) exact-and-sparse-solve. Delivers the F-EVO-6 open item (block-tridiagonal Cholesky + width certificate).
code_changes: src/junction_tree.rs new module (JunctionTreeCholesky + Clique + balanced_binary_tree) + 3 tests
open_questions: contiguous-storage rewrite + wall-clock criterion benchmark (analytic flops -> measured time)
optimized refactorize_path (recompute only the leaf->root path) = the incremental online solver
non-additive / data-scarce cross-clique target (block loses materially, MF/dense hold)
route the separator message literally through hg_message on a signed hypergraph

findings.claim: The value of the cross-clique (separator) coupling is a FUNCTION OF DATA DENSITY, and it is large exactly when data is scarce. Discriminating test (kato15, 5 seeds, branching clique tree depth 6, d=441, 63 cliques, clique arity ~8): sweeping measurements-per-clique `per`, the R2 gap MF-minus-BLOCK (separator-dropping) rises from 0.089 (per=2) to a PEAK 0.159 at per=6 (below arity, data-scarce), then falls to 0.006 at per=40 (data-rich, the E5 regime). MULTIFRONTAL R2 == DENSE R2 at every (per,seed). Weight-recovery RMSE confirms: BLOCK mis-estimates the separator variables (per=6: 0.615 vs MF 0.492) because it sees only their residual clique's data while MF pools every clique that shares them. This closes the block-vs-info question open since E3: E5's tiny ~0.0002 gap was a data-rich REGIME property, not a method limit; the coupling is worth up to ~0.16 R2 when data is scarce.
findings.classification: MEASUREMENT (resolves an open question; no new component)
findings.source_experiment: evolvent E6 (examples/evolvent_multifrontal.rs --per sweep, kato15 5 seeds x 7 densities)
findings.confidence: high (5 seeds x 7 densities; monotone-then-peak trend consistent across seeds)
findings.scope: additive-in-features linear target; the scarcity is genuine data-scarcity (per < clique arity), NOT a manufactured non-additive coupling; single depth (6). The gap peaks just below clique arity and vanishes when data-rich.
findings.limitations: the gap is a property of the (structure, data-density) pair, not of MF alone -- MF exactness is regime-independent, block-diagonal is nearly as good AND cheaper in the rich regime; separator-sharing axis (sep, depth) and a genuinely non-additive cross-clique feature untested.
findings.framework_consequence: No new component -- uses JunctionTreeCholesky::solve vs solve_block_diagonal. Resolves F-EVO-5/6/7 open question 'a non-additive/data-scarce cross-clique target where BLOCK loses materially': ANSWERED via data-scarcity (block loses up to 0.16 R2). Quantifies WHEN to pay for the coupling (keep it when data-scarce; block-diagonal suffices when rich). Added regression coverage for solve_block_diagonal (single_clique_equals_dense).
code_changes: src/junction_tree.rs single_clique_equals_dense extended to cover solve_block_diagonal
open_questions: separator-sharing axis (sep, depth) -- gap should grow with more cliques per separator
a genuinely non-additive cross-clique feature (explicit product spanning a separator) -- does the gap widen beyond data-scarcity?
contiguous-storage rewrite + wall-clock benchmark (E5 follow-up)

findings.claim: Separator-SHARING is a THRESHOLD effect, not a dose-response. Star topology (one separator of 3 vars shared across `fanout` children => fanout+1 cliques per separator), fixed data-scarcity (per=4, child arity 6), 5 seeds kato15: the block-diagonal deficit is LARGE (~0.16-0.28 R2 median) whenever a separator is shared, materially more than minimal-sharing fanout=1 (0.10) and comparable to E6's within-tree gap -- BUT it does NOT grow monotonically with fanout: the gap JUMPS at fanout 1->2 then PLATEAUS across fanout 2..32 (medians 0.184,0.162,0.228,0.278,0.189, IQRs heavily overlapping). MF R2 == DENSE at every cell. PARTIAL REFUTATION of the stated hypothesis 'more cliques per separator -> monotonically bigger gap': sharing MATTERS (confirmed), degree-of-sharing beyond 2 does NOT add much (refuted). Mechanism (inferred): at fixed scarcity the residual vars cap both solvers, and block's per-measurement error on the shared separator is set once fanout>=2 -- more children add equally-sep-affected measurements, not more per-measurement error.
findings.source_experiment: evolvent E7 (examples/evolvent_multifrontal.rs --fanout star topology, kato15 6 fanouts x 5 seeds)
findings.confidence: medium-high (5 seeds; the PLATEAU/non-monotonicity is clear, the exact plateau level is noisy -- IQRs overlap, small-d noise dominates fanout=1)
findings.scope: additive-in-features linear target; single scarcity (per=4) and separator width (sep=3); small d at low fanout (d=9 at fanout=1 is noisy -- one seed had BLOCK beat MF via over-regularization). Plateau conclusion rests on fanout>=2.
findings.limitations: does NOT test a genuinely non-additive cross-clique feature (the last open discriminating knob); the joint scarcity x sharing sweep at larger d untested; single-seed (seed 0) looked monotone -- multi-seed is the verdict.
findings.framework_consequence: No new component (uses star_clique_tree + existing solve/solve_block_diagonal). REFINES F-EVO-8: the coupling's value is a THRESHOLD on sharing (turns on when a separator is shared) bounded by the per-clique data budget, NOT a dose-response in the number of sharers. New topology helper star_clique_tree (shared-separator) + regression test.
open_questions: a genuinely non-additive cross-clique feature (explicit product spanning a separator)
joint scarcity x sharing sweep at larger d (shed small-d noise)
contiguous-storage rewrite + wall-clock (E5 follow-up)

findings.claim: A genuinely NON-ADDITIVE cross-clique term is a REPRESENTABILITY limit, not an estimation gap. Target y = Sum_k[w0*rk0 + w1*rk1 + beta*(rk0*rk1)] + w_h*h; the product prod_k=rk0*rk1 is fittable ONLY by a clique wide enough to contain both operands. Measured (Mac, 5 seeds x 5 beta, K=12 triples, width-4 star clique tree): MF-WIDE (multifrontal hosting the product) R2 == DENSE-WIDE R2 EXACTLY at every (beta,seed), flat ~0.998 regardless of beta, at 14% of dense storage. NARROW (linear-only, product omitted) COLLAPSES as beta grows: 0.999 -> 0.926 -> 0.748 -> 0.376 -> 0.060 -- and this is the DATA-RICH regime (per=40), so more data does NOT close it: the term is simply absent from the narrow hypothesis space. The required treewidth = the target's interaction order (a degree-2 interaction needs width >= 3); the SBSH bounded-width certificate is exactly the check that the tree's width covers the data's interaction order -- the validity precondition for the whole E4-E7 line.
findings.classification: NEW_CANONICAL_CAPABILITY (measurement; closes the non-additive question, distinct axis from F-EVO-8/9)
findings.source_experiment: evolvent E8 (examples/evolvent_width.rs, Mac 5 seeds x 5 beta)
findings.confidence: high (5 seeds; MF-WIDE==DENSE-WIDE is an algebraic identity, NARROW collapse is monotone and large across all seeds)
findings.scope: explicit product feature (linear-in-features model; the clique tree makes the interaction affordable+exactly-solvable at bounded width, does NOT discover it); degree-2 interaction, single arity. Rich regime (per=40) -- deliberately NOT scarcity.
findings.limitations: the non-additive term must be an explicit feature; NARROW's collapse is because the feature is absent from its basis (honest content of 'too narrow'); higher-order interactions (degree>=3 -> width>=order+1) untested; beta is a controlled dial, not fit from real data.
findings.framework_consequence: No new component (reuses JunctionTreeCholesky + InfoEvolventHead). CLOSES the non-additive-cross-clique open question of F-EVO-5/6/7/9: it does NOT touch the E6/E7 estimation plateau -- it is a DISTINCT axis (representability = treewidth vs interaction order). Establishes the width certificate as the VALIDITY precondition for the exactness line: the multifrontal solver handles non-additive terms exactly at O(d*w^3) iff width >= interaction order. Separates the two mechanisms cleanly: estimation gap (E6, closes with data) vs representability gap (E8, closes with width).
regression_tests: (reuses E5 junction_tree exactness tests; example is the integration evidence)
open_questions: higher-order interactions (degree>=3 -> width>=order+1)
wire the SBSH width certificate as an explicit precondition check on JunctionTreeCholesky::new
contiguous-storage rewrite + wall-clock (E5 follow-up)

findings.claim: WALL-CLOCK validation (criterion, §10) of the E5/F-EVO-7 analytic flop win: the multifrontal solve (JunctionTreeCholesky::solve) vs dense Gaussian (InfoEvolventHead::solve, identical answer) on the branching tree, d 105->889. MEASURED speedup 5.9x -> 16.4x -> 63x -> 255x -- grows exactly as the O(d*w^3) vs O(d^3) asymptotic predicts (same (d/w)^2 growth as the flop count), CONFIRMING the direction. BUT the analytic flop count (18x-1270x) OVERSTATED the deployable speedup by a stable ~5x (flop/measured -> 5.0x for large d): the dense baseline is a contiguous cache-friendly d*d array while the multifrontal chases many small per-clique Vecs (allocation + pointer-chase) and re-allocates frontals each solve. At d=889: 85us vs 21.8ms (255x, online-viable). The flop count is the CEILING a contiguous-storage rewrite would approach.
findings.classification: VALIDATION_WITH_CORRECTION (measurement; corrects the magnitude of F-EVO-7's flop claim)
findings.confidence: high (criterion 30 samples, tight CIs ~1-2%; ratios stable across the size sweep)
findings.scope: single machine (Mac arm64); dense baseline is Gaussian elimination (~2x flops of pure Cholesky) so the ratio is a mild over-estimate of the pure-algorithmic win; both solvers re-factorize from scratch each solve.
findings.limitations: absolute us are machine-specific (ratios transfer); a dense-CHOLESKY baseline would narrow the ratio by up to ~2x; the contiguous-storage rewrite that would close the ~5x flop-to-measured gap is NOT built.
findings.framework_consequence: CORRECTS F-EVO-7 and the JunctionTreeCholesky registry note: cite MEASURED 5.9x-255x as the deployable speedup, the 18x-1270x flop count as the CEILING. Direction and growth of F-EVO-7 stand; magnitude corrected downward ~5x. Gives the contiguous-storage rewrite a measured target (~5x on the table). Closes the 'analytic flops not wall-clock' caveat repeated across E5-E8.
regression_tests: (benchmark, not a correctness test; solver exactness already guarded by E5 junction_tree tests)
open_questions: contiguous-storage multifrontal (one arena) -> close the ~5x gap toward the flop ceiling
dense-Cholesky baseline for the pure-algorithmic ratio
wall-clock of the incremental refactorize_path once built
